perturbation.original_text: 14. DISPUTES. In the event of any dispute which arises between the parties and which relates to the subject matter of this Agreement, the parties acknowledge and agree that any such dispute shall be submitted for binding arbitration in accordance with the arbitration procedures established by the American arbitration Association in Denver, Colorado. If such association is not then in existence, an independent association of arbitrators may be utilized which is designated by agreement of the parties. In the event the parties are unable to agree on an independent association of arbitrators, either party may apply to a court of competent jurisdiction for appointment of arbitrators.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. GOVERNING LAW. This Agreement shall be construed and enforced in accordance with, and governed by the laws of the State of Colorado without regard to conflicts of laws principles. 14. DISPUTES. In the event of any dispute which arises between the parties and which relates to the subject matter of this Agreement, the parties acknowledge and agree that any such dispute shall be submitted for binding arbitration only if all parties explicitly agree to arbitration at the time the dispute arises. If all parties do not consent to arbitration, either party may pursue remedies in a court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement mandated binding arbitration. The modified version makes arbitration contingent on mutual agreement at the time of the dispute, effectively nullifying the original mandatory arbitration clause and opening the door to litigation. This weakens the enforceability of arbitration and introduces ambiguity.
perturbation.law_explanation: The FAA favors enforcement of valid arbitration agreements. By making arbitration dependent on future consent, the modified clause undermines the enforceability that the FAA seeks to protect, and does not explicitly state that the arbitration clause is valid and enforceable.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: 9 U.S. Code § 2 - Validity, irrevocability, and enforcement of agreements to arbitrate | U.S. Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Quick search by citation: Title Section Go! 9 U.S. Code § 2 - Validity, irrevocability, and enforcement of agreements to arbitrate U.S. Code Notes prev | next A written provision in any maritime transaction or a contract evidencing a transaction involving commerce to settle by arbitration a controversy thereafter arising out of such contract or transaction, or the refusal to perform the whole or any part thereof, or an agreement in writing to submit to arbitration an existing controversy arising out of such a contract, transaction, or refusal, shall be valid, irrevocable, and enforceable, save upon such grounds as exist at law or in equity for the revocation of any contract or as otherwise provided in chapter 4. (July 30, 1947, ch. 392, 61 Stat. 670 ; Pub. L. 117–90, § 2(b)(1)(A) , Mar. 3, 2022 , 136 Stat. 27 .) Derivation Act Feb. 12, 1925, ch. 213, § 2, 43 Stat. 883 . Editorial Notes Amendments 2022— Pub. L. 117–90 inserted “or as otherwise provided in chapter 4” before period at end. Statutory Notes and Related Subsidiaries Effective Date of 2022 Amendment Amendment by Pub. L. 117–90 applicable with respect to any dispute or claim that arises or accrues on or after Mar. 3, 2022 , see section 3 of Pub. L. 117–90 , set out as an Effective Date note under section 401 of this title . U.S. Code Toolbox Law about... perturbation.justification: The changed text contradicts the original agreement regarding dispute resolution, specifically concerning arbitration. The original text mandated binding arbitration, while the modified text makes arbitration contingent on mutual agreement at the time the dispute arises. This directly undermines the enforceability of the original arbitration agreement. The scraped law snippet from 9 U.S. Code § 2 states that agreements to arbitrate are generally "valid, irrevocable, and enforceable." By making arbitration dependent on future consent, the modified clause weakens this irrevocability and enforceability, presenting a structural flaw that generates a legal contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5. RETENTION OF ENDORSEMENT RIGHTS. Except with respect to endorsement rights granted to the Company for the apparel described herein, the Professional shall retain all rights in and to the professional's name and endorsement and the Professional shall not be prevented from using, permitting or licensing others to use the Professional name or endorsement in connection with the advertising, promotion or sale of any product other than golf apparel as described in Section 1 hereof. The Professional represents and warrants that no agreement, contract or understanding exists which would prevent or limit the performance of any of his obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 13. HEADINGS. The headings in this Agreement are for purposes of convenience and easy reference only and shall not limit or otherwise affect the meaning hereof. 5. RETENTION OF ENDORSEMENT RIGHTS. Except with respect to endorsement rights granted to the Company for the apparel described herein, the Professional shall retain all rights in and to the professional's name and endorsement and may be prevented from using, permitting or licensing others to use the Professional name or endorsement in connection with the advertising, promotion or sale of any product. The Professional represents and warrants that no agreement, contract or understanding exists which would prevent or limit the performance of any of his obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text clearly states the professional retains endorsement rights for products other than golf apparel. The modified text removes the phrase 'other than golf apparel', making it ambiguous and implying the Company may have rights to restrict the professional from endorsing other products, creating a conflict of interest and potentially limiting the professional's earning potential. Additionally, the phrase 'shall not be prevented' has been changed to 'may be prevented' which entirely changes the meaning of the sentence, directly impacting the professional's rights.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Colorado law regarding implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing.
perturbation.law_explanation: Colorado law implies a covenant of good faith and fair dealing in every contract. By creating ambiguity that allows the Company to potentially restrict the Professional's endorsement rights outside of golf apparel, the modification could violate this implied covenant, as it can be interpreted as the Company acting in bad faith to limit the Professional's ability to earn income.

perturbation.justification: The modified text contradicts the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing, as argued in the LLM-generated Law Explanation. By removing the phrase 'other than golf apparel' and changing 'shall not be prevented' to 'may be prevented,' the company now has the potential ability to restrict the professional's endorsement rights outside of golf apparel, which could be viewed as an act of bad faith.

perturbation.original_text: 6. TERM. The term of this Agreement shall extend from the date of execution hereof through and until _______, unless extended by written agreement of the parties. The parties shall use their best efforts to reach agreement on the terms of any such extension. In the event the Professional should for any reason become a non-exempt PGA Tour player, the Company shall have the right to terminate this Agreement at its discretion at any time during the initial term or any extension thereof. Additionally, if either part shall fail to observe or perform any of the agreements or obligations undertaken by such party hereunder, and such failure or default shall continue for a period of 30 days following notice from the non-defaulting party to the defaulting party during which such failure or default shall not have been cured by the defaulting party, then the non-defaulting party shall have the right to terminate this Agreement following the expiration of such 30-day notice period. The non-defaulting part hereunder. Following the expiration of 90 days from the termination or expiration of this Agreement, the Company shall cease usage of all publicity, promotion and advertising materials which contain the Professional's Image, it being the understanding of the parties that during such 90-day period the Company shall have the right to use such remaining publicity, promotion or advertising materials as shall then be available to the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. SERVICES OF THE PROFESSIONAL. The Professional agrees to perform the following services during the term of this Agreement: a. The Professional agrees that, with respect to men's apparel, he will exclusively endorse and use exclusively in play, practice, exhibits, clinics and other events open to the media or public, Haley brand apparel which shall consist of shirts, vests, jackets, sweaters, pants and shorts (if permitted by applicable rules). 6. TERM. The term of this Agreement shall extend from the date of execution hereof through and until _______, unless unilaterally terminated by either party. The parties shall not be required to use their best efforts to reach agreement on the terms of any such extension. In the event the Professional should for any reason become a non-exempt PGA Tour player, the Company shall have the right to terminate this Agreement at its discretion at any time during the initial term or any extension thereof. Additionally, if either part shall fail to observe or perform any of the agreements or obligations undertaken by such party hereunder, and such failure or default shall continue for a period of 30 days following notice from the non-defaulting party to the defaulting party during which such failure or default shall not have been cured by the defaulting party, then the non-defaulting party shall have the right to terminate this Agreement following the expiration of such 30-day notice period. The non-defaulting part hereunder. Following the expiration of 90 days from the termination or expiration of this Agreement, the Company shall continue usage of all publicity, promotion and advertising materials which contain the Professional's Image, indefinitely, as the company sees fit.
perturbation.explanation: The original termination clause requires a 30-day notice period and an opportunity to cure a default. The modified clause allows either party to unilaterally terminate the agreement, removing the notice and cure provisions. Furthermore, the clause stating the Company will cease using publicity, promotion and advertising materials which contain the Professional's Image has been modified to allow the Company to continue using the materials indefinitely. This is contradictory because the original clause states that either party may only terminate the contract if the other does not perform their obligations. This suggests the parties intended the contract to be enforced absent these conditions, but this modification suggests the contract is at will, and may be terminated by either party at any time for no reason, which is a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Colorado law regarding good faith and fair dealing in contract termination.
perturbation.law_explanation: While Colorado Revised Statutes § 4-1-304 directly addresses good faith under the UCC, the Restatement (Second) of Contracts § 205 provides a broader understanding of the principle applied to contracts generally. The modification to allow unilateral termination without cause or notice, and to allow indefinite use of the Professional's Image conflicts with the implied duty of good faith and fair dealing because it deprives the Professional of the reasonably expected benefits of the agreement, since the Company is now free to exploit the Professional's Image in perpetuity.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: good faith | Wex | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you good faith Good faith is a broad term that’s used to encompass honest dealing. Depending on the exact setting, good faith may require an honest belief or purpose, faithful performance of duties , observance of fair dealing standards, or an absence of fraudulent intent . A fiduciary relationship creates a duty of good faith between the agent and the principal . The breach of this duty of good faith can lead to liability . Failure to act in good faith is known as bad faith and is generally considered to be a level of culpability greater than negligence . [Last reviewed in January of 2023 by the Wex Definitions Team ] Wex COMMERCE business law contracts wex definitions business sectors legal education and practice Wex Toolbox
perturbation.justification: The modified text contradicts the implied duty of good faith and fair dealing. The original contract included provisions designed to protect both parties, such as a 30-day notice period for default and an agreement to cease using the Professional's image after 90 days. The modified text allows either party to unilaterally terminate the agreement without notice and permits the Company to use the Professional's image indefinitely, depriving the Professional of reasonably expected benefits. The scraped law snippet states, "good faith may require an honest belief or purpose, faithful performance of duties, observance of fair dealing standards, or an absence of fraudulent intent." The modification arguably violates the 'observance of fair dealing standards' because the company can exploit the professional's image indefinitely with no reciprocal benefit or control.
